name: Secured Visa Card
effective_cost: Free — no annual fee, but earns nothing and requires a refundable $300–$5,000 deposit

verdict.text: No rewards at all — the Cash+ Secured or Altitude Go Secured earn the exact same way for the exact same deposit and $0 fee

earn_note: This is the bare-bones version of U.S. Bank's secured lineup — same deposit range and $0 annual fee as Cash+ Secured and Altitude Go Secured, but no cash back or points at all. There's no earn-rate reason to pick this over either of those unless you specifically want the simplest possible product.

points.note: Nothing to redeem. Value is purely the credit-building mechanism, not any earning.

transfer_partners.highlight: No rewards program — nothing to transfer.

insurance.detail: Not responsible for unauthorized transactions once reported
Not publicly documented for this card
Not publicly documented for this card
Not publicly documented for this card

protection_note: No purchase-protection or extended-warranty guide published for this card.
rental_note: No auto rental coverage documented publicly for this card.

services.name: Refundable security deposit
services.detail: $300–$5,000, held in a secured savings account, sets your credit limit dollar-for-dollar.

services.name: Free credit score access
services.detail: TransUnion VantageScore via the U.S. Bank mobile app and online banking.

services.name: Choose your due date
services.detail: Pick a payment due date that fits your schedule.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Their activity is reported to the credit bureaus too, on the same account
No rewards earned — this card has no rewards program to share

additional_cards.note: Free to add, and since this account reports to all three bureaus, an authorized user can benefit from the payment history even though there's no rewards program to share.
